blogs.title: Slavkovsky is set to breakout next season
blogs.body: Slavkovsky just came out of an impressing season at the pro level. He finished the season with an impressive 20 goals season for 50 points. Who would of thought after Christmas, that he would be a 20 goals scorer, let alone having 50 points. I am very optimistic for next season and I can't wait to see what magic Kent Hughes has for us in the off season. Top 6 winger? Martin Necas? Who knows, but I am so excited about next season!
blogs.author: William C.

blogs.title: Caufield expected to reach 50 goals next year
blogs.body: Caufield required a shoulder surgery that ended his first season as a pro. He finished the season with 26 goals. We were pretty optimistic that he would be able to regain that form this season and that he would easily beat his career high. Easily surpass 30 goals, maybe even get 40 goals. But, it was completely the opposite. WOMP WOMP. His shot was not reminiscing of last year Caufield. He started the campaign slow. Didn't put up a large amount of goals. We were all disapointed. However, after the all-star break, something clicked. He was different. He shot came back and he went on a streak in the last 15 games and he was able to finsih the campaign with a career high of 28 goals! Next year I expect that he beat his goals total of this year.
blogs.author: Khaliun B.

blogs.title: Suzuki 80 points next year?
blogs.body: Suzuki, what a beauty! Thank you Bergevin, this was one of your smartest move, but we don't miss you one bit. It has been more than a decade that the Canadiens had a 70 points scorer. A very long time, if you tell me. So, what is next? Even though we finished in the bottom of the league, I still feel that this year it was a successful campaign. We lost Dach after 2 games into the season and the injury bug happened again this year, but it was a very positive campaign! Our captain was almost a point per game. Caufield didn't miss any games this year and looks to be a more complete player rather than a unidimensional player, Slavkovsky exploded after the all star break and our goalies are showing growth. I am hopeful for next year. A lot of players had career years like Newhook who replaced Dach's void. Might be biased, but I think next year, we will miss the playoff by not much!
blogs.author: William C.
